name: Kara Hahn
subject: Demand Congress Pass Equal Pay for Equal Work
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kara Hahn for Congress Kara Hahn for Congress
body:
|
---

[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43257410/336412037/922507959?refcode=em21_emailheader&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDc2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NjIxMmQ4NC0xYmFhLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_j3OhoHopHGlVs14phZshJE-ShqbSZCJ6atbEO5kBvQ=)

Team,

We need to keep fighting for equal pay.

Last week, people across the country marked Equal Pay Day – and it was energizing to see so many calls for change on such an important issue. But if we're going to make real progress, we need more than one day to show why equal pay is so critical – and so urgent.

This is a moment when costs are rising on essentials: healthcare, housing, childcare, eldercare, and so much more. It's unconscionable that families have to face those increases while women are making less money for every hour they work.

**[Now is the time to step up. So, I'm asking you to join me today in showing your support. The Equal Pay for Equal Work Act is before Congress NOW. Will you sign your name, and join me in calling for its passage?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43257411/336412051/-609160141?ms=em22_220322_equalpay&refcode=em22_220322_equalpay&noskip=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDc2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NjIxMmQ4NC0xYmFhLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_j3OhoHopHGlVs14phZshJE-ShqbSZCJ6atbEO5kBvQ=&emci=0e265f99-06aa-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&emdi=86212d84-1baa-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&ceid=25369896)**

As someone who spent years as a single mom, I know how hard it can be to make ends meet – especially when everything from groceries to gas is getting more expensive. With all these extra costs, unequal pay makes getting by much more challenging.

We're also just beginning to emerge from a period of economic challenges that fell especially hard on working women, particularly women of color. Millions of women left the workforce during the pandemic. As the economy continues to bounce back, women shouldn't be put at a disadvantage.

It's well past time to say enough is enough.

**[We can rise to the challenge of this unprecedented time, close the wage gap and give families more of what they need to get by. Can you join me and urge Congress to pass the Equal Pay for Equal Work Act?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43257412/336412058/-609160141?ms=em22_220322_equalpay&refcode=em22_220322_equalpay&noskip=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDc2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NjIxMmQ4NC0xYmFhLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_j3OhoHopHGlVs14phZshJE-ShqbSZCJ6atbEO5kBvQ=&emci=0e265f99-06aa-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&emdi=86212d84-1baa-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&ceid=25369896)** [SIGN THE PETITION](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43257413/336412060/-609160141?ms=em22_220322_equalpay&refcode=em22_220322_equalpay&noskip=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDc2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NjIxMmQ4NC0xYmFhLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_j3OhoHopHGlVs14phZshJE-ShqbSZCJ6atbEO5kBvQ=&emci=0e265f99-06aa-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&emdi=86212d84-1baa-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&ceid=25369896) And of course, this isn't just about the challenges of the moment.

I have two daughters. My oldest daughter is just getting her career off the ground and my younger daughter is working hard in school as she thinks about her future. They deserve to know that they'll earn equal pay for equal work. ALL our daughters deserve a future where their work will be valued equally with their male colleagues. And they should see that we're taking action to make it happen.

**[I'm urging Congress to pass the Equal Pay for Equal Work Act, and I hope you'll stand with me. Can you sign your name to call for equal pay?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43257414/336412062/-609160141?ms=em22_220322_equalpay&refcode=em22_220322_equalpay&noskip=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDc2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NjIxMmQ4NC0xYmFhLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_j3OhoHopHGlVs14phZshJE-ShqbSZCJ6atbEO5kBvQ=&emci=0e265f99-06aa-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&emdi=86212d84-1baa-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&ceid=25369896)** [ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43257415/336412064/-609160141?ms=em22_220322_equalpay&refcode=em22_220322_equalpay&noskip=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDc2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NjIxMmQ4NC0xYmFhLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_j3OhoHopHGlVs14phZshJE-ShqbSZCJ6atbEO5kBvQ=&emci=0e265f99-06aa-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&emdi=86212d84-1baa-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&ceid=25369896)
Thank you,

Kara Hahn

| Kara Hahn is a lifelong Long Islander, social worker, county legislator, and candidate for Congress in NY-01. This open seat is a _must win_ for Democrats to defend our majority in the House and continue driving progress in America, but we can't do it without your help. **Make a recurring donation to sustain our movement for the long haul and send Kara to Congress!** [DONATE MONTHLY](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43257416/336412065/189849860?refcode=em22_220322_equalpay&recurring=1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDc2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NjIxMmQ4NC0xYmFhLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_j3OhoHopHGlVs14phZshJE-ShqbSZCJ6atbEO5kBvQ=) [DONATE ONCE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43257417/336412067/189849860?refcode=em22_220322_equalpay&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDc2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NjIxMmQ4NC0xYmFhLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_j3OhoHopHGlVs14phZshJE-ShqbSZCJ6atbEO5kBvQ=)

subject: Did you hear what he did?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Georgette Gómez for Assembly 2022 Special Election, #1443884
body:
https://georgettegomez.org/ [https://georgettegomez.org/]
A big story just broke about a former San Diego Councilman — and Georgette’s opponent — David Alvarez.
In 2019, Alvarez lobbied with San Diego Gas & Electric to build a dangerous power station in Barrio Logan. He did this one year after leaving office, which likely violates city ethic laws and certainly violates the trust of the community.
https://voiceofsandiego.org/2022/03/21/alvarez-work-for-sdge-could-have-violated-city-ethics-law/ [https://voiceofsandiego.org/2022/03/21/alvarez-work-for-sdge-could-have-violated-city-ethics-law/]
Rather than work for the best interests of his constituents, Alvarez bows to corporate interests. Georgette, on the other hand, has spent her career standing up to big corporations and keeping pollutants out of our neighborhoods.
Is it any wonder that Big Oil and other corporate interests are now pouring money into Alvarez's campaign?
Our election is right around the corner. Can you chip in now to support Georgette so she can keep fighting for our families? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/georgette_gomez_for_assembly_2022_special_election?refcode=X_032222_Alvarez]
Thank you so much!
-- Team Gómez
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/georgette_gomez_for_assembly_2022_special_election?refcode=X_032222_Alvarez [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/georgette_gomez_for_assembly_2022_special_election?refcode=X_032222_Alvarez]

5445 Madison Avenue
Sacramento, CA 95841
United States

| Team, it’s clear that the AZ GOP isn’t going to wait to see the outcome of their lawsuit calling for an end to all early voting and mail voting in Arizona before coming up with a plan B.

That’s because the Trump-endorsed Republican candidate for governor responded to the lawsuit saying **_she supports the lawsuit – and an end to early voting – completely!_**

Early voting and mail-in voting have been a staple of Arizona elections for three decades. But thanks to Trump’s lies, AZ Republicans are trying to end all early voting and mail voting. _If they continue to hold onto the governor’s office and the AZ legislature, we could see a complete end to our voting rights!_

**[Team, we have to defend voting rights like early voting and PEVL, and we can’t let the AZ GOP win statewide offices that will allow them to end these rights. Can you donate $10 today to help us fight back and protect voting rights?](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953z3LNGdjjp_6L5R-butU81DC_GBshmIF8mx9OFUrjz3av5xFA5CkTXnx3pynijl4MKeU6cpzk0i0EDigXnIF-evgnUouxCjexzb5COT9e77ff9hTDNFFF6pbohKT8jA41k9YFDvVmG2veyA-fAWmBnGSaiK8r-jlXkby2IUsKCR2S7A70l7oeFoq5blEcnWBR4c8kutQimlikH92rJQ4wOx71B_u00r-btM9DdkZENBHcSTaAziQbdS1tOi0bs2C-EP5U0OaJCebV9QqaOhyrCWWMB5cfGuU06Qs4PnMo37-ShH0iUds-DQHIhLFsj_gKV8CTOEW2SPU8YTsk7TJeZtC_osEaKSPEV4Y3x4PBv_HzJ-nmnKm9l2BYydvxgkCgjpbbdB6lv_i8K4HVNIO51MH2DdUutExwzGXjYsHh_Xfu4UJS6jBBY4eXmCSEj6MCvZCWqda6d9ppZ4zU5DVwapI6DIu9OF8wbvGeWdvWMltOKJsaGMjGRy6U3zRchxYdw/3kk/mRAUZgRxRsOshlqtMG5f8A/h1/hHUg0_81mN-fBBnbyiXrGCtTzuv1Qqsvf6TgRltXETc)** [

Polling and experts both say we have the best chance we’ve had in more than a decade to **_FLIP_** the Arizona governor’s office.

Now, the stakes are raised even more when Republican Kari Lake wants to end all voting that isn’t done on election day.

**_Nevermind the fact that Lake has voted by mail several times before!_**

clean_body: | | | We have to fight back | | | Team, it’s clear that the AZ GOP isn’t going to wait to see the outcome of their lawsuit calling for an end to all early voting and mail voting in Arizona before coming up with a plan B. That’s because the Trump-endorsed Republican candidate for governor responded to the lawsuit saying she supports the lawsuit – and an end to early voting – completely! Early voting and mail-in voting have been a staple of Arizona elections for three decades. But thanks to Trump’s lies, AZ Republicans are trying to end all early voting and mail voting. If they continue to hold onto the governor’s office and the AZ legislature, we could see a complete end to our voting rights! Polling and experts both say we have the best chance we’ve had in more than a decade to FLIP the Arizona governor’s office. Now, the stakes are raised even more when Republican Kari Lake wants to end all voting that isn’t done on election day. Nevermind the fact that Lake has voted by mail several times before! If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: Help us fight back, AZ Dems For up-to-date COVID-19 information and guidance, visit Paid for by the Arizona Democratic Party and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. subject: My Recent Q & A With Fellow New Mexicans
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Block for NM](http:// "Paid for by Block for NM")
body:
What Inspires Me | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/mcshanellc/mask-mandates-6890545?e=102b599530)
---
| | [ ](https://blockfornewmexico.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=05a2cec40e7a4755c4df899c3&id=2daa34443e&e=102b599530)
---
| | Peter,
---
| I recently had the great honor of joining fellow New Mexicans for a Q & A event so that candidates could share answers to challenging questions about life in general and where they stand on the issues. **I was asked: What inspires me?**

_I shared that personal stories inspire me and will never forget where I came from._ I haven’t forgotten the experiences that I’ve gone through in life. As a man, I will exhibit compassion with the emotional muscle that has grown from my experiences in life. I hope that my service will inspire others to enter public service for their community.
**As we practice resilience during these trying times, I believe the bond we build as warriors together helps us to persevere through adversity for victory. **
I shared three different moments in my life when I experienced inspiration, and you can watch me share that in this video [here](https://blockfornewmexico.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=05a2cec40e7a4755c4df899c3&id=bb468ec216&e=102b599530):
---
| [ ](https://blockfornewmexico.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=05a2cec40e7a4755c4df899c3&id=470624797e&e=102b599530)
---
| Once was when I was traveling down the road in Kabul, Afghanistan. I saw a young girl dressed in a uniform walking to school. It inspired me that she was going to school, and I reflected with humility that I might have played a small part in that.

I’ve been inspired by my grandfather, a World War II veteran and one of the wisest men I’ve ever known. He had an eighth-grade education and worked hard through difficult times to raise four children.

I also shared that I have been inspired to watch my mother recover from drug addiction and alcoholism.

**The adversity we have faced in life helps build resilience and strength to face struggles ahead.** We all face personal battles, and this similarity can unite us to face the toughest of challenges together as fellow Americans.
**LET’S PURSUE A SEASON OF HEALING IN OUR NATION TOGETHER**
_**As your next Governor,** I will remain **grounded,** **grateful,** and **humble** about my past experiences so that I **never** lose touch of the struggles being faced by the people of New Mexico_. I have faith that our resilience will overcome the hardships resulting from the inhumane leadership we have experienced these past few years. Your support **in a donation of any amount today** will show the establishment politicians that our triumphant spirit of freedom will prevail!
---

name: Welch for Vermont
subject: While President Biden and other Democrats won big in the 2020 election, Vermont’s Republican governor won reelection by 41 points
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY WELCH FOR VERMONT
body:
If Republicans win back the majority, they’ll bring all our progress to a screeching halt.

# [](https://act.welchforvermont.com/go/144?t=2&akid=146%2E61531%2ExRdqmE)
Hello,
Mitch McConnell and national Republicans see Vermont’s Senate seat as a “sleeper” race for November’s midterms.
Here’s why they see a potential win here: while President Biden and other Democrats won big in the 2020 election, Vermont’s Republican governor won reelection by 41 points—**the largest margin of victory in a Vermont governor’s race in nearly 25 years.**
There’s a lot on the line: protecting our democracy, combating climate change, and lowering the price of prescription drugs all hinge on Democratic control of the Senate. And with national Republicans already talking about spending big to defeat Peter, we need to fight back.
[**What we raise will be reported publicly at the end of the month. Can you pitch in right away to show everyone watching that Vermont isn’t for sale?**](https://act.welchforvermont.com/go/1656?t=3&akid=146%2E61531%2ExRdqmE)
_If you’ve saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will process automatically:_

---
| [$3](https://act.welchforvermont.com/go/1656?t=14&amount=3&akid=146%2E61531%2ExRdqmE)
---
| [$15](https://act.welchforvermont.com/go/1656?t=16&amount=15&akid=146%2E61531%2ExRdqmE)
---
| [$28](https://act.welchforvermont.com/go/1656?t=18&amount=28&akid=146%2E61531%2ExRdqmE)
---
| [$50](https://act.welchforvermont.com/go/1656?t=20&amount=50&akid=146%2E61531%2ExRdqmE)
---
| [$100](https://act.welchforvermont.com/go/1656?t=10&amount=100&akid=146%2E61531%2ExRdqmE)
---
| [OTHER](https://act.welchforvermont.com/go/1656?t=12&akid=146%2E61531%2ExRdqmE)
---

---
This open seat in Vermont could be the difference between passing our positive progressive agenda and Mitch McConnell controlling the Senate again.
If Republicans win back the majority, they’ll bring all our progress to a screeching halt—and they’re already talking about putting Social Security, Medicare, and the Affordable Care Act on the chopping block.
But by winning in Vermont, we can help keep national Republicans like Mitch McConnell out of power.
[**Donate $15 or whatever you can to help elect Peter as the next senator from Vermont. Let’s keep Vermont, and the Senate, blue.**](https://act.welchforvermont.com/go/1656?t=7&akid=146%2E61531%2ExRdqmE)
Thanks for your support!
Team Welch

---
| [DONATE ](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pwfv-em-22mar?refcode=em_20220322_f_t_gop&refcode2=146_61531_xRdqmE&akid=146%2E61531%2ExRdqmE)

A governor is supposed to pass laws that keep people safe — but DeSantis is hell-bent on doing just the opposite. He’s expected to sign one of the _most restrictive abortion laws in the entire country._ He supports the Don’t Say Gay bill, which criminalizes any discussion of gender identity and sexual orientation in schools. Instead of doing what’s best for Floridians, he’s trying to keep his name in the headlines as much as possible before his inevitable run for the presidency.

**Ron, Florida isn’t your playground and you don’t get to jeopardize our lives with your politics of division.**

But friend, the reality is that my campaign is the only thing standing in the way of DeSantis’s dangerous “lawmaking,” So together, let’s make sure he can’t bring any more harm to Floridians by voting him out.
[**_With so much at stake — healthcare, education, climate change and more — we need to defeat Ron DeSantis and protect the future of Florida. Can you make a contribution to my campaign today?_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43263330/336457158/1828375930?refcode=20220322_f_2_ND_b&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTYxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkY2EwYzRlMi00M2FhLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sgaC1YQx-WiYFWQxKyyeLDj8dyNdo4zqQhKPP87lr1A=)

Charlie Crist

name: PramilaForCongress.com
subject: Cancel it
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
NO ONE should go into crushing debt for pursuing higher education.
#
Peter,
ICYMI last week: Alongside activists and colleagues in the Congressional Progressive Caucus, Pramila called for more than a dozen executive actions the White House can take _on their own_ to deliver for working families.
One of the most popular actions Pramila and progressives are calling for the president to take is student loan debt cancellation. And while reports indicate that the White House will once again extend the pandemic-related pause on federal loan payments, it’s simply not enough. **We need to cancel every last penny of student debt, once and for all.**
[**NO ONE should go into crushing debt for pursuing higher education. If you’re with Pramila in this fight, chip in $3 or any amount to fuel our organizing today:**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-20220322?refcode=em20220322-1127&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&refcode2=1127_420207_LvGgiq&akid=1127%2E420207%2ELvGgiq)
**[Contribute $3 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-20220322?refcode=em20220322-1127&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=20&refcode2=1127_420207_LvGgiq&akid=1127%2E420207%2ELvGgiq)**
Private lenders are lobbying tirelessly for loan payments to resume. **If they do, working people will pay an average of $460/month in student debt payments — a burden that, according to recent polling from Student Debt Crisis and Savi, most borrowers are not financially secure enough to deal with again.**

[**But Pramila needs your support to keep up this grassroots fight against the lobbyists and corporations profiting from student loan debt. clean_body: NO ONE should go into crushing debt for pursuing higher education. Peter, ICYMI last week: Alongside activists and colleagues in the Congressional Progressive Caucus, Pramila called for more than a dozen executive actions the White House can take on their own to deliver for working families. One of the most popular actions Pramila and progressives are calling for the president to take is student loan debt cancellation. And while reports indicate that the White House will once again extend the pandemic-related pause on federal loan payments, it’s simply not enough. We need to cancel every last penny of student debt, once and for all. Private lenders are lobbying tirelessly for loan payments to resume. If they do, working people will pay an average of $460/month in student debt payments — a burden that, according to recent polling from Student Debt Crisis and Savi, most borrowers are not financially secure enough to deal with again. Democrats control the House, the Senate, and the White House. To lift people up — and into the future — we need to use that power to deliver on a transformative economic agenda. One key component to that agenda is forgiving student debt — and President Biden can get it done today. In solidarity, Team Jayapal | | Email us: info@pramilaforcongress.com

name: Team AOC
subject: Understanding no-fly zones
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
The term ‘no-fly zone’ has been used a lot recently, but what does that really mean?
# [](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/143?refcode=em20220322-2884-header&t=1&akid=2884%2E2526796%2EGLeVFQ)
There are a lot of pundits, journalists, and members of Congress using the term ‘no-fly zone’ right now, but what does that mean?
## No-Fly Zones Explained...
Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky is urging NATO to impose a no-fly zone over Ukraine. Simply put, a no-fly zone is airspace where certain aircraft are not allowed to enter.1
In this instance, a no-fly zone would be used to ban Russian aircraft from entering Ukrainian air space to launch attacks, transport troops and weapons, and conduct surveillance.2
## Why is this dangerous?
**Declaring a no-fly zone is essentially tantamount to a declaration of war.** This is due to the enforcement that a no-fly zone requires.
Meaning that, if a no-fly zone is established, the U.S. and NATO may have no choice but to shoot down Russian aircraft that violate the no-fly zone directly.
**This would ultimately result in an escalation between nuclear powers, with the potential for a full fledged war in Europe.**
## Here’s the issue...
The lack of transparency surrounding what a no-fly zone _actually_ does leaves the majority of Americans unaware of the potential risks an action like this could have.
Equating a no-fly zone with war is by no means a controversial statement amongst foreign policy experts.3 **The fact is, if you’re not comfortable with the U.S. declaring war on Russia, you’re probably not comfortable with the U.S. or NATO initiating a no-fly zone.**
[Alexandria is fighting for honest and transparent foreign policy, so that Americans understand the potential risks and consequences behind these important decisions. If you can, please consider making a donation to keep Alexandria in Congress.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/aoc-email-20220322?refcode=em20220322-2884-P_FND_EXP_LEG&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&refcode2=2884_2526796_GLeVFQ&akid=2884%2E2526796%2EGLeVFQ)

Team AOC

clean_body: We need to show Resilience | Friends, Let me cut to the chase - WE ARE LOSING GROUND. As it stands right now, Liberal Democrat Cheri Beasley is outraising me, while I am focused on winning this Primary. If we don’t keep up with her pace, we will start the General significantly behind. Can you chip in to help us make a comeback? | President Trump endorsed me because he knows that I’m the best man for the job. But first, we have to defeat RINO Pat McCrory or we can kiss Trump’s legacy goodbye. As it stands right now, we have a lot of work to do. But if you will re-up your commitment now, we can keep that from happening. I NEED YOU NOW MORE THAN EVER! God bless, Ted Budd | Ted Budd is a father, small business owner, and conservative leader running for Senate in North Carolina. He is the ONLY candidate who is endorsed by President Trump and the only candidate who will fight for the America First Agenda in Washington. body:
We could have lost OVER $115,000,000
Team –

It’s been just over one year since the American Rescue Plan (ARP) was signed into law – a plan that **MAGA Loyalist Mike Garcia voted against. [If you’re ready for a representative who puts you first, chip in $5 right now to flip this district blue >>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43258915/336419829/27414565?refcode=em_res_2022.03.22_b1_fca27b_all&amount=5&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDY3NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ODY4YWU4OS0yNGFhLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=AL2e-kESpsUL-L-GyLSS7aKPMhV7RsgoBhAT5xwR8ro=)** | ******[FLIP CA-27 BLUE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43258916/336419830/27414565?refcode=em_res_2022.03.22_b1_fca27b_all&amount=5&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDY3NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ODY4YWU4OS0yNGFhLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=AL2e-kESpsUL-L-GyLSS7aKPMhV7RsgoBhAT5xwR8ro=)******
---
The ARP helped families across the nation recover from the devastating economic and health effects of the pandemic. **Garcia would rather play political games** than help his constituents here in California’s 27th district. **If it went his way, we would have lost out on _over $115 million dollars_** for expenses like public safety, public health, health care, human services, and similar employees, including first responders.

clean_body: We could have lost OVER $115,000,000 Team – It’s been just over one year since the American Rescue Plan (ARP) was signed into law – a plan that MAGA Loyalist Mike Garcia voted against. = | ****=** The ARP helped families across the nation recover from the devastating economic and health effects of the pandemic. Garcia would rather play political games than help his constituents here in California’s 27th district. If it went his way, we would have lost out on over $115 million dollars for expenses like public safety, public health, health care, human services, and similar employees, including first responders. It’s time for this district to be represented by someone like Quaye Quartey , who served this country for two decades as an intelligence officer in the Navy, and is ready to serve once again by advocating for the hard-working families of this district in Congress. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = – Flip CA-27 Blue | Retired Naval Commander John Quaye Quartey II felt a call to serve his country once again after he watched extremists carry out a Trump-fueled insurrection at our nation’s Capitol. body:
I have not and I will not take a penny of special interest money.

| Dear Peter Willis,
This is Republican Brian Burley - I am running to be our Youngest America First Congressman in the country!
I am running against Radical Katie Porter. Porter and her far left friends are targeting this seat as Porter has drifted over to our seat which has historically been a conservative stronghold! Porter is funded by every special interest west of the Mississippi and I am the America First Conservative that will defend our Republican seat! I am the fighter we need in Congress that will fight for our faith, families and freedoms. I have taken on the establishment and special interests before as I have worked tirelessly to Start A New Conservative Movement in California!

name: Lena Moffitt, Evergreen Action
subject: Our next direct action [SIGN ON]
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY EVERGREEN ACTION
body:
If we act now, we can close a polluter loophole.
| [](https://act.evergreenaction.com/letter/tell-SEC-close-polluter-loopholes/?utm_source=ema_2022-03-22_SEC_comment_&source=ema_2022-03-22_SEC_comment_&t=2&akid=12956%2E53494%2E5GY_7b) | The SEC just proposed a new climate rule—but it includes major loopholes for polluters. [**_Will you sign an official public comment calling on the SEC to strengthen their new draft climate disclosure rule right now?_**](https://act.evergreenaction.com/letter/tell-SEC-close-polluter-loopholes/?utm_source=ema_2022-03-22_SEC_comment_&source=ema_2022-03-22_SEC_comment_&t=3&akid=12956%2E53494%2E5GY_7b)
---

| [TAKE ACTION NOW](https://act.evergreenaction.com/letter/tell-SEC-close-polluter-loopholes/?utm_source=ema_2022-03-22_SEC_comment_&source=ema_2022-03-22_SEC_comment_&t=4&akid=12956%2E53494%2E5GY_7b)
---
Friend,
If a company is engaging in risky business, its investors deserve to know—and there's nothing more risky than fueling the climate crisis.
That's why this news is a huge deal: The Securities and Exchange Commission just proposed a rule that would require companies to disclose how much greenhouse gas pollution they emit and the extent of their exposure to climate-related financial risks. As the climate crisis continues to upend business as usual, this rule will arm investors with the vital information they need to protect their financial futures.
**But it's not all good news. The proposed rule leaves it to the companies to determine whether or not they need to disclose their entire carbon footprint, and shields them from liability if they lie on their annual filings. These are massive loopholes that could allow polluters to hide the majority of their emissions.**
Luckily, the SEC just opened the public comment window for this proposed rule. This is our chance to flex our grassroots power and demand they strengthen this rule before the deadline closes. [**_Will you sign an official public comment right now? We'll submit it on your behalf directly to SEC decision-makers!_**](https://act.evergreenaction.com/letter/tell-SEC-close-polluter-loopholes/?utm_source=ema_2022-03-22_SEC_comment_&source=ema_2022-03-22_SEC_comment_&t=5&akid=12956%2E53494%2E5GY_7b)
[SUBMIT AN OFFICIAL COMMENT](https://act.evergreenaction.com/letter/tell-SEC-close-polluter-loopholes/?utm_source=ema_2022-03-22_SEC_comment_&source=ema_2022-03-22_SEC_comment_&t=6&akid=12956%2E53494%2E5GY_7b)
---
Think about the 2008 financial crisis. Millions of Americans didn't realize their money was tied up in high-risk subprime mortgage investments—and they were hit hardest when the bubble burst.
**Right now, millions of Americans have no way of knowing if their retirement funds or college savings plans are exposed to climate risk.** It's up to the SEC to enforce full transparency of climate risks and give investors the information they need to make climate-smart decisions.
[**_We have limited time to act. Will you sign an official public comment right now? Just sign your name, and we'll take care of the rest—and together, we'll ensure that the SEC hears our input loud and clear._**](https://act.evergreenaction.com/letter/tell-SEC-close-polluter-loopholes/?utm_source=ema_2022-03-22_SEC_comment_&source=ema_2022-03-22_SEC_comment_&t=7&akid=12956%2E53494%2E5GY_7b)
Thank you for taking direct action today,
Lena Moffitt
Chief of Staff, Evergreen Action
P.S. In recent weeks, we submitted **2,500** official comments to the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency and **6,617** official comments to the Bureau of Land Management. [**_Can you help us continue the trend and beat our latest record?_**](https://act.evergreenaction.com/letter/tell-SEC-close-polluter-loopholes/?utm_source=ema_2022-03-22_SEC_comment_&source=ema_2022-03-22_SEC_comment_&t=8&akid=12956%2E53494%2E5GY_7b)
[SUBMIT AN OFFICIAL COMMENT](https://act.evergreenaction.com/letter/tell-SEC-close-polluter-loopholes/?utm_source=ema_2022-03-22_SEC_comment_&source=ema_2022-03-22_SEC_comment_&t=9&akid=12956%2E53494%2E5GY_7b)
---

---

subject: FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE: Governor Larry Hogan Endorses Kelly Schulz For Governor
body:
| | | |

For Immediate Release:
March 22, 2022
Contact:
410-305-9172
[press@kellyschulzforgovernor.com](mailto:press@kellyschulzforgovernor.com?subject=&body=)

---
**Governor Larry Hogan Endorses** **Kelly Schulz For Governor**

---
_Schulz Served Seven Years In The Hogan Administration, First Female Secretary of Both Department of Labor and Department of Commerce_

---
**ANNAPOLIS, MD** — Today, during a press event with campaign supporters in Annapolis, Governor Larry Hogan formally endorsed Kelly Schulz to succeed him as the next governor of Maryland. Schulz who served in the Hogan administration for seven years as the first female secretary of both the Department of Labor and Department of Commerce played a central role in Changing Maryland for the Better.

“If you approve of how we have led Maryland, then Kelly Schulz is your choice for governor,” **said Governor Hogan.** “With all the progress we have made together, we cannot afford to go back to the days of 43 consecutive tax hikes with jobs and businesses fleeing to other states, and Kelly is the only candidate in this race running to continue moving Maryland in the right direction. She will fight to hold violent criminals accountable, cut taxes, and make sure our children can get the world-class education they deserve.”

**Governor Hogan continued:** “As a small business owner and Delegate, Kelly Schulz has a proven track record of creating jobs and getting things done. As a member of our cabinet, Kelly was an integral part of our success over the past seven years. I’ve seen firsthand how much she loves our state and how hard she will work for all of our people. I am proud to support her campaign to keep Changing Maryland for the Better.”

“It is a great honor for me to receive the support of Governor Hogan,” **said Kelly Schulz.** “The governor has been a transformational leader for our state and I thank him for entrusting me with continuing to build upon the tremendous success that our state has experienced and his legacy of accomplishment.”
**Schulz went on to say:** "Our campaign is about making sure Maryland stays safe, steady, and prosperous while focusing on the issues that Marylanders really care about. Governor Hogan has proven what is possible when you make a commitment to commonsense solutions and not partisan politics. If Marylanders want smart, pragmatic leadership in Annapolis, that is exactly what I am pledging to continue."

Prior to Governor Hogan’s election in 2014, a majority of Marylanders were unhappy with the direction of the state. After 40 consecutive tax increases and an anti-business approach from state government, nearly half of all Marylanders stated that they would flee the state if they could. Since Governor Hogan took office, those attitudes have changed – Maryland is now open for business, and in poll after poll, the vast majority of Marylanders approve of the direction of the state.

Over the last eight years, Governor Hogan has been tested with the riots in Baltimore and confronting a worldwide pandemic, as well as a personal bout with cancer. He’s overcome these challenges and Marylanders have overwhelmingly approved of his job performance, awarding him with reelection and making him among the most popular elected officials in America.

Under the governor’s leadership, and with Kelly Schulz as one of his trusted advisers, Maryland has become a better place to live, work, and raise a family. Tasked by Governor Hogan to serve as Maryland’s chief regulatory and economic development officer, Schulz was instrumental in working to grow Maryland’s economy and foster a more competitive business environment.

While working for the Hogan administration, Schulz partnered with the governor to advance a number of key initiatives. Working in tandem, Governor Hogan and Kelly Schulz fought for tax relief and advocated for the largest tax cut for small businesses in state history, the RELIEF Act, eliminated or streamlined hundreds of regulations, and established and grew nationally recognized job training and apprenticeship programs. Because of their work together, Maryland now ranks as the most improved state for business.

Prior to serving in the Hogan administration, Kelly Schulz was a successful small business owner and delegate who represented Frederick County. As a member of the General Assembly, Schulz introduced or co-sponsored over $250 million in targeted tax relief that was enacted for Marylanders and focused on keeping communities safe, while expanding economic and educational opportunities for all Marylanders.

As Maryland’s next governor, Kelly Schulz is committed to ensuring that Marylanders will have strong leadership in the governor’s office so that our state can be safe, steady, and prosperous.
---
-###-

clean_body: | | | | For Immediate Release: March 22, 2022 Contact: 410-305-9172 mailto:press@kellyschulzforgovernor.com?subject=&body= Governor Larry Hogan Endorses Kelly Schulz For Governor Schulz Served Seven Years In The Hogan Administration, First Female Secretary of Both Department of Labor and Department of Commerce ANNAPOLIS, MD — Today, during a press event with campaign supporters in Annapolis, Governor Larry Hogan formally endorsed Kelly Schulz to succeed him as the next governor of Maryland. Schulz who served in the Hogan administration for seven years as the first female secretary of both the Department of Labor and Department of Commerce played a central role in Changing Maryland for the Better. “If you approve of how we have led Maryland, then Kelly Schulz is your choice for governor,” said Governor Hogan. “With all the progress we have made together, we cannot afford to go back to the days of 43 consecutive tax hikes with jobs and businesses fleeing to other states, and Kelly is the only candidate in this race running to continue moving Maryland in the right direction. She will fight to hold violent criminals accountable, cut taxes, and make sure our children can get the world-class education they deserve.” Governor Hogan continued: “As a small business owner and Delegate, Kelly Schulz has a proven track record of creating jobs and getting things done. As a member of our cabinet, Kelly was an integral part of our success over the past seven years. I’ve seen firsthand how much she loves our state and how hard she will work for all of our people. I am proud to support her campaign to keep Changing Maryland for the Better.” “It is a great honor for me to receive the support of Governor Hogan,” said Kelly Schulz. “The governor has been a transformational leader for our state and I thank him for entrusting me with continuing to build upon the tremendous success that our state has experienced and his legacy of accomplishment.” Schulz went on to say: "Our campaign is about making sure Maryland stays safe, steady, and prosperous while focusing on the issues that Marylanders really care about. Governor Hogan has proven what is possible when you make a commitment to commonsense solutions and not partisan politics. If Marylanders want smart, pragmatic leadership in Annapolis, that is exactly what I am pledging to continue." Prior to Governor Hogan’s election in 2014, a majority of Marylanders were unhappy with the direction of the state. After 40 consecutive tax increases and an anti-business approach from state government, nearly half of all Marylanders stated that they would flee the state if they could. Since Governor Hogan took office, those attitudes have changed – Maryland is now open for business, and in poll after poll, the vast majority of Marylanders approve of the direction of the state. Over the last eight years, Governor Hogan has been tested with the riots in Baltimore and confronting a worldwide pandemic, as well as a personal bout with cancer. He’s overcome these challenges and Marylanders have overwhelmingly approved of his job performance, awarding him with reelection and making him among the most popular elected officials in America. Under the governor’s leadership, and with Kelly Schulz as one of his trusted advisers, Maryland has become a better place to live, work, and raise a family. Tasked by Governor Hogan to serve as Maryland’s chief regulatory and economic development officer, Schulz was instrumental in working to grow Maryland’s economy and foster a more competitive business environment. While working for the Hogan administration, Schulz partnered with the governor to advance a number of key initiatives. Working in tandem, Governor Hogan and Kelly Schulz fought for tax relief and advocated for the largest tax cut for small businesses in state history, the RELIEF Act, eliminated or streamlined hundreds of regulations, and established and grew nationally recognized job training and apprenticeship programs. Because of their work together, Maryland now ranks as the most improved state for business. Prior to serving in the Hogan administration, Kelly Schulz was a successful small business owner and delegate who represented Frederick County. As a member of the General Assembly, Schulz introduced or co-sponsored over $250 million in targeted tax relief that was enacted for Marylanders and focused on keeping communities safe, while expanding economic and educational opportunities for all Marylanders. As Maryland’s next governor, Kelly Schulz is committed to ensuring that Marylanders will have strong leadership in the governor’s office so that our state can be safe, steady, and prosperous. -###- | "Facebook" | "Twitter" | instagram.com/kellymschulz "Instagram" ---|---|--- Kelly Schulz for Governor P.O. Box 6528, Annapolis, MD 21401 Authority: Kelly Schulz for Governor, Inc. Treasurer: Sam Malhotra

Team,
Tomorrow afternoon, Mike DeWine will be giving the 2022 State of the State address – his first since 2019, before the COVID-19 pandemic.
Here’s what I’m betting he won’t talk about:
* The Ohioans who have been left behind as politicians choose complacency over investing in the jobs of the future
* The scandals and pay-to-play politics that have plagued our state government under Republican control
* The need to protect reproductive rights at a time when they are increasingly under attack at all levels of government

**[Mike DeWine is out of touch with our state – it’s time we elected a governor who will put Ohioans first. Will you chip in $5 to my campaign ahead of his speech tomorrow night?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43263433/336464467/-576363077?refcode=nw_em_220322_1_t2&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&city=&state=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODM5MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NjIyMTIzMy00OGFhLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=nmTCg3Ix5mbnYOIsNYK77Q5QaDFI3rb96I0aYxn3HVA=)**
Republican politicians in Columbus have been so focused on protecting their own interests that they’re leaving Ohio families in the dust.
I know that by investing in companies that invest in Ohio, prioritizing family-first policies and programs like paid leave and fully-funded public education systems, and putting Ohio’s people at the heart of all we do, we can build an Ohio where all can thrive, not just the wealthy few.
But I need you with me to make that vision a reality.
**[Mike DeWine is determined to take us backwards – and Ohio deserves better. Please, rush $5 to help send Mike packing this year.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43263434/336464468/-576363077?refcode=nw_em_220322_1_t2&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&city=&state=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODM5MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NjIyMTIzMy00OGFhLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=nmTCg3Ix5mbnYOIsNYK77Q5QaDFI3rb96I0aYxn3HVA=)**

Friends --

I’m going to let you in on a little secret:

**March is a TOUGH month for fundraising.** The election feels far away, and people want to tune politics out. I get it.

But as our campaign manager, I can’t relax. March is also our most critical month for fundraising. As we speak, we are deciding how many organizers we can hire, how many TV ads we can run and how many campaign offices we can open. And at the end of the month, all of our fundraising numbers for the quarter will be made public.

And if we don’t raise $2,000 more by midnight to get us back on track for our monthly goal, I’m going to be forced to make some tough cuts.

**[So I really need you to make a donation today and help me turn this around. clean_body: | = Friends -- I’m going to let you in on a little secret: March is a TOUGH month for fundraising. The election feels far away, and people want to tune politics out. I get it. But as our campaign manager, I can’t relax. March is also our most critical month for fundraising. As we speak, we are deciding how many organizers we can hire, how many TV ads we can run and how many campaign offices we can open. And at the end of the month, all of our fundraising numbers for the quarter will be made public. And if we don’t raise $2,000 more by midnight to get us back on track for our monthly goal, I’m going to be forced to make some tough cuts. = | = = = = = = We can’t do this without you, Grace Fleming Campaign Manager, Pfaff for Congress | | Paid for by Pfaff for Congress This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com Pfaff for Congress PO Box 3606 La Crosse, WI 54602 United States If you would like to receive only our most important emails, If you no longer wish to receive emails please .

Listen,

It is deeply unfortunate that candidates who only take the time to connect with wealthy high dollar donors are rewarded. As a result, we are left with elected officials who are fighting for the interests of their wealthy donors and not the most vulnerable among us.

_That’s why we’re so proud to run a campaign fueled by over 7,494 individual donors– with 1,743 of those from February alone!_

This proves individual donations from people like you, who want to see change, who want to see grassroots organizers like Nida elected to office, is how we will win this race.

**But despite having more than twice as many donations than our opponents, wealthy establishment donors and corporate PACs are starting to outpace our grassroots fundraising.** [**_We know we have the people power and that our small-dollar donors can close the gap. Dear Peter,

Our End-of-Quarter deadline is quickly approaching. And let me tell you, the stakes have never higher.
We need to show a MASSIVE amount of financial strength to stop Bloomberg, Steyer, and Soros from coming after me.
Fighting the good fight doesn't come without its consequences. Because of my lawsuits defending American energy independence, protecting our 1st and 2nd Amendments, and fighting for election integrity, these guys are COMING AFTER ME.
They'll be looking at our report as soon as it is public. But, if we can show a high enough fundraising number, they'll know they can't mess around.
That's why I'm reaching out to you today, Peter. I need your help!
We are still about $50,000 short of our big goals, which is why we are activating a **500% MATCH TODAY ONLY**. _But,_ you must _contribute by 11:59PM TONIGHT._

clean_body: | | | | You know what time of year it is. Dear Peter, Our End-of-Quarter deadline is quickly approaching. And let me tell you, the stakes have never higher. We need to show a MASSIVE amount of financial strength to stop Bloomberg, Steyer, and Soros from coming after me. Fighting the good fight doesn't come without its consequences. Because of my lawsuits defending American energy independence, protecting our 1st and 2nd Amendments, and fighting for election integrity, these guys are COMING AFTER ME. They'll be looking at our report as soon as it is public. But, if we can show a high enough fundraising number, they'll know they can't mess around. That's why I'm reaching out to you today, Peter. I need your help! We are still about $50,000 short of our big goals, which is why we are activating a 500% MATCH TODAY ONLY. But, you must contribute by 11:59PM TONIGHT. Can I count on your support by TONIGHT? | | | | | | Remember, ONLY the Attorneys General can block Biden's and Congress' illegal federal overreach right now. That's why this quarter is so important. We have to show strength or the far-left billionaires will hinder our ability to DEFEND AMERICA. Sincerely, Patrick Morrisey West Virginia Attorney General | Follow us on Social Media ****|****|****|****|** Paid for by TEAM MORRISEY, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and to benefit of Morrisey for Attorney General, Morrisey for West Virginia, Inc., Blue and Gold Fund, and West Virginia Republican Party, Inc. c/o Red Curve Solutions 138 Conant Street, 2nd Floor Beverly, MA 01915. Copyright (C) 2021 Team Morrisey. All rights reserved. Mailing Address** Team Morrisey P.O. Box 11903 Charleston, WV 25339-1903 = \- ==

| | Hi Peter, I will keep this brief: Moms in Office is falling short of our quarterly fundraising goal and we are counting on you to help us close the gap before our most important deadline just a week away. There is too much at stake to fall behind at this stage in an election year. Moms in Office is working tirelessly to provide targeted support programs for candidates with caregiving responsibilities that will make it possible for them to win big. **[Will you be one of the 176 donors needed from your area to chip in before our end-of-quarter deadline?](https://click.everyaction.com/k/43259658/336423782/600483780?refcode=0930&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4MjY1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjRhOTczYTZmLTI5YWEtZWMxMS05OTdlLTI4MTg3OGI4M2Q4YSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=V-EulQ5vJ7l78TxagC15yBU1jBePLGOerRJyWkJ5l7w=)** We are the **only women's organization helping to pay for childcare** on the campaign trail. Our PAC made a significant investment by establishing a Childcare Fund that our candidates can use to pay for their caregiving expenses. [**Be one of the 176 or more supporters needed from your area by chipping in what you can and help us fully fund our Childcare Program. We are counting on you to help us reach our most important fundraising goal by our end-of-the-quarter deadline on March 31st.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/43259659/336423783/600483780?refcode=0930&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4MjY1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjRhOTczYTZmLTI5YWEtZWMxMS05OTdlLTI4MTg3OGI4M2Q4YSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=V-EulQ5vJ7l78TxagC15yBU1jBePLGOerRJyWkJ5l7w=) Any amount that you are able to donate will have a huge impact on our year-round fundraising work and it will keep our support programs going that are so important for our candidates' success. Thank you! Simona Grace Founder and Executive Director Moms in Office

clean_body: There is too much at stake for us to fall behind and with just a week until our most important deadline Today, we are counting on you. | | | | | Hi Peter, I will keep this brief: Moms in Office is falling short of our quarterly fundraising goal and we are counting on you to help us close the gap before our most important deadline just a week away. There is too much at stake to fall behind at this stage in an election year. Moms in Office is working tirelessly to provide targeted support programs for candidates with caregiving responsibilities that will make it possible for them to win big. = We are the only women's organization helping to pay for childcare on the campaign trail. Our PAC made a significant investment by establishing a Childcare Fund that our candidates can use to pay for their caregiving expenses. = Any amount that you are able to donate will have a huge impact on our year-round fundraising work and it will keep our support programs going that are so important for our candidates' success. Thank you! Simona Grace Founder and Executive Director Moms in Office I will chip in to pay for childcare! = = = = = | | | | | | ---|---|---|--- Moms in Office 13351 Riverside Drive #265 Sherman Oaks, CA 91423 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please . | | | **_Learn More About Chuck_**

---
---
| | | Family, hard work and community. These are the things that have defined me as a person more than anything else and are the things that make New Hampshire a special place. Several local Salem community leaders became role models and mentors and got me involved with helping our community. With their encouragement, I ran for Salem Selectman and won. I ran for Town Moderator and won. Then I ran for State Representative and served for two terms from 1998-2002. I was elected to the State Senate in 2002 and was appointed to the Finance Committee. My experience running a business, meeting a payroll, and keeping a conservative budget helped prepare me for the challenges of crafting a balanced state budget. I quickly became known as one of the state’s leaders in budgeting and tax policy. I took a step back from politics in 2006. My daughter was starting her junior year in high school and Susan and I spent most of the next few years traveling to her field hockey and basketball games. We were her biggest fans. In 2010, I was reelected to the Senate. When I returned to the Finance Committee, I found the financial mess that the Democrats had put our state in. The state had an $800 million deficit. We quickly turned that around and put the state’s finances back on a sustainable path. For my work, colleagues gave me the greatest honor of my life — electing me the Senate President in 2013. In 2020, I led Republican efforts to retake the majority in the State Senate. New Hampshire was the only state in the country that was able to flip a blue state legislature to red and it was largely due to our strong fight against the Democrats’ proposed state income tax. Last year, we passed one of the most conservative and fiscally responsible budgets that New Hampshire has ever seen. It is balanced, cuts taxes for small businesses, provides more than $300 million in property tax relief, bans late-term abortions, and prohibits the use of discriminatory teaching in our schools. It puts New Hampshire on a path to long-term prosperity. The work ethic, commitment to family and sense of community that I learned at a young age have stuck with me as an adult and drive everything I do. I still wake up at 4am every morning to work at our business before going to Concord to do the people’s business. More often than not, I still take my first phone calls of the day while driving a tractor. Washington, D.C. has enough silver-spoon, Ivy League lawyers. I believe that I am a product of all the best parts of New Hampshire — the spirit of freedom, sense of volunteerism and responsibility of giving back to the community that has helped shape me into the person I am today. I want to bring New Hampshire to Washington and makes sure our voices are heard. I want to bring New Hampshire values to a place that needs them now more than ever. I want to work for you and I hope you will consider electing me to represent you in the U.S. Senate.
_[The End of Quarter Deadline is coming up next week. Contribute here before March 31st.](https://click.mlsend2.com/link/c/YT0xOTExODYxMTY0MDkwODU4MDE3JmM9eDJ1MiZlPTAmYj05NDcwMDY5NzYmZD1tM2o2dzlt.6EOi6AymGi8TMBHqGErFCTg1SFziDJAXkrdsUfVd7fU)_

I quickly became known as one of the state’s leaders in budgeting and tax policy. I took a step back from politics in 2006. My daughter was starting her junior year in high school and Susan and I spent most of the next few years traveling to her field hockey and basketball games. We were her biggest fans. In 2010, I was reelected to the Senate. When I returned to the Finance Committee, I found the financial mess that the Democrats had put our state in. The state had an $800 million deficit. We quickly turned that around and put the state’s finances back on a sustainable path. For my work, colleagues gave me the greatest honor of my life — electing me the Senate President in 2013. In 2020, I led Republican efforts to retake the majority in the State Senate. New Hampshire was the only state in the country that was able to flip a blue state legislature to red and it was largely due to our strong fight against the Democrats’ proposed state income tax. Last year, we passed one of the most conservative and fiscally responsible budgets that New Hampshire has ever seen. It is balanced, cuts taxes for small businesses, provides more than $300 million in property tax relief, bans late-term abortions, and prohibits the use of discriminatory teaching in our schools. It puts New Hampshire on a path to long-term prosperity. The work ethic, commitment to family and sense of community that I learned at a young age have stuck with me as an adult and drive everything I do. I still wake up at 4am every morning to work at our business before going to Concord to do the people’s business. More often than not, I still take my first phone calls of the day while driving a tractor. Washington, D.C. has enough silver-spoon, Ivy League lawyers. I believe that I am a product of all the best parts of New Hampshire — the spirit of freedom, sense of volunteerism and responsibility of giving back to the community that has helped shape me into the person I am today. I want to bring New Hampshire to Washington and makes sure our voices are heard. I want to bring New Hampshire values to a place that needs them now more than ever. I want to work for you and I hope you will consider electing me to represent you in the U.S. Senate. | | | | | | | | | | | Paid for by Friends of Chuck Morse | | | Friends of Chuck Morse | | PO Box 106 North Salem, NH 03073 | You received this email because you signed up on our website or made a purchase from us.

body:
This is scary, Peter
| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43260057/336425995/-1540418300?code=jtk1179-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzc2OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmOGY2MzI2Yy0yYmFhLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=uKdN_jkDgl-sgJDjLF-aNBsosCcDhiK2pVc6iN8yeWU=)

This is scary, Peter.

The GOP is growing increasingly radical – especially in Texas. Right next door to us, Gov. Greg Abbott is pushing a far-right agenda like we've never seen before.
In just the last year, Gov. Abbott has targeted communities of color with voter suppression laws, enacted a near-total ban on abortion, and pushed some of the worst anti-LGBTQ policies in the country.
**As Gov. Abbott doubles down on his far-right agenda, it's our duty to reject his radicalism. [That's why I created this petition to condemn Gov. Abbott: Will you add your name before midnight to speak out against his attacks on personal freedom?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43260058/336425997/1042735105?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsurveys.signforgood.com%2Fmlg-stop-extremist-gop-survey&code=jtk1179-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzc2OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmOGY2MzI2Yy0yYmFhLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=uKdN_jkDgl-sgJDjLF-aNBsosCcDhiK2pVc6iN8yeWU=&emci=e4ad5668-36a9-ec11-a22a-281878b85110&emdi=f8f6326c-2baa-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&ceid=24265352)**

_DEADLINE: 11:59 P.M. TONIGHT_
**Over the last few months, we've heard horrifying stories from Texans about how Gov. Abbott's oppressive agenda has impacted their lives:**
* **Abortion ban:** A woman in Texas was at risk of dying during a miscarriage. Doctors recommended an abortion – but the law didn't allow it. She had to take an emergency flight out of the state in order to survive.

* **Anti-LGBTQ policies:** Gov. Abbott is sending Family and Protective Services to investigate parents – leaving these families terrified of being split up by the state.

* **Fear of what's next: **There are hundreds of thousands of heartbreaking stories like these coming out of Texas right now, and the Texas GOP will only get worse if we don't take a stand.

**That's why I need your help. It's up to us to stop GOP radicalism, and I'm counting on 50,000 concerned Americans like you to speak out against Gov. Abbott's far-right agenda before my petition closes at midnight:**

name: Maggie Hassan
subject: When I was young
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
body:
#

[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mek-em-hassan-mar22?refcode=em220322-2-15&t=1&refcode2=2406_6688874_8FVOPi&akid=2406%2E6688874%2E8FVOPi)
Hi there,
When I was young, I didn't think I would grow up to be the second woman in American history to be elected both Governor and Senator. But it has been the honor of my lifetime to represent Granite Staters.
I ran for office to make sure everyone was counted in our country’s shared success. In the Senate, **I strive to make our democracy more inclusive and more accessible each and every day – and I'm dedicated to continuing this work.**
But here's the deal: **Mitch McConnell only has to flip one seat to take back the Senate, and I'm at the top of his list.** That's why National GOP groups are doing whatever they can to beat me – spending over $7.5 million against me, rallying out-of-state dark money groups, and recruiting extreme Republicans to run against me.
I hate asking for money, but I urgently need your help to win this November. [**So will you please split a donation between my campaign and Mark Kelly’s so that we can defend this seat and hold control of the Senate?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mek-em-hassan-mar22?refcode=em220322-2-15&t=2&refcode2=2406_6688874_8FVOPi&akid=2406%2E6688874%2E8FVOPi)

name: Max Steiner
subject: re: protecting our forests in CA-01
disclaimer_text: paid for by his donors in Pacific Gas & Electric (PG +E) and the lumber industry
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43260697/336428960/390307162?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTYyOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxMjEwNjU4Ni0yZGFhLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VUwT4S18NxSA0DPJ5s_xKnxZ_pk0szuyfrVXA-oOimA=)
HI there,

It’s International Day of Forests.

Forests are hugely important worldwide. They capture and store carbon while creating oxygen we need to breathe. More than that, they are also beautiful. Overall, forests take up around 31% of the world’s land area.

Forests are even more important here in California's 1st District. We have nearly 10 million acres of forest. Of that, the U.S. Forest Service manages 5,000,000 acres, private owners own 4,000,000 acres, and the Bureau of Land Management, National Park Service, and assorted state agencies own a little under 1,000,000 acres. That’s almost 60% of our congressional district!

Unfortunately, with this bounty comes risk. Climate change and poor forest management have turned our forests into tinderboxes. Massive fires have ripped across our district -- including the Camp Fire which destroyed Paradise, and the Carr Fire that destroyed my brother’s home in Redding. Even among those of us that have not lost our homes, many have been evacuated, and all of us have lost days to poor air quality from the smoke.

It’s time to proactively manage our forests. That means thinning small-diameter trees, subsidizing biomass facilities to convert dangerous fuel loads to renewable electricity, and scaling up Native American practices for regular, low-risk prescribed fires.

**My opponent, Doug LaMalfa, wants to return to clear-cutting. Doug LaMalfa is bought and paid for by his donors in Pacific Gas & Electric (PG +E) and the lumber industry. We can’t trust him to solve anything.**

**[My campaign isn’t taking money from PG+E. Instead, we rely on small donors like you. On this International Day of Forests, please consider throwing my campaign a few “greenbacks” to bring the green back to our North State Forests.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43260698/336428961/360059203?refcode=em-3.22.22-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTYyOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxMjEwNjU4Ni0yZGFhLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VUwT4S18NxSA0DPJ5s_xKnxZ_pk0szuyfrVXA-oOimA=)**

Have a good weekend,

Max
| **[Donate »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43260699/336428962/360059203?refcode=em-3.22.22-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTYyOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxMjEwNjU4Ni0yZGFhLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VUwT4S18NxSA0DPJ5s_xKnxZ_pk0szuyfrVXA-oOimA=)**
---

clean_body: | = HI there, It’s International Day of Forests. Forests are hugely important worldwide. They capture and store carbon while creating oxygen we need to breathe. More than that, they are also beautiful. Overall, forests take up around 31% of the world’s land area. Forests are even more important here in California's 1st District. We have nearly 10 million acres of forest. Of that, the U.S. Forest Service manages 5,000,000 acres, private owners own 4,000,000 acres, and the Bureau of Land Management, National Park Service, and assorted state agencies own a little under 1,000,000 acres. That’s almost 60% of our congressional district! Unfortunately, with this bounty comes risk. Climate change and poor forest management have turned our forests into tinderboxes. Massive fires have ripped across our district -- including the Camp Fire which destroyed Paradise, and the Carr Fire that destroyed my brother’s home in Redding. Even among those of us that have not lost our homes, many have been evacuated, and all of us have lost days to poor air quality from the smoke. It’s time to proactively manage our forests. That means thinning small-diameter trees, subsidizing biomass facilities to convert dangerous fuel loads to renewable electricity, and scaling up Native American practices for regular, low-risk prescribed fires. My opponent, Doug LaMalfa, wants to return to clear-cutting. Doug LaMalfa is bought and paid for by his donors in Pacific Gas & Electric (PG +E) and the lumber industry. We can’t trust him to solve anything. = Have a good weekend, Max | = | = Max Steiner is a combat veteran, foreign service officer, and Democrat running to flip California's 1st District from red to blue. Consider supporting Max's campaign by = If you'd like to receive less email, add your name We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to . Max Steiner is a combat veteran of the Iraq War and Sergeant First Class in the Army Reserves. Use of his military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of Defense. Max Steiner for Congress 2485 Notre Dame Blvd, Ste 370 #194 Chico, CA 95928 United States

body:
Take a look.
#
**Our last end-of-quarter deadline before our May 17th primary election is just over a week away** — and what we raise will become public for our opponents, the media, and political pundits to see.
We’re racing to reach our critical fundraising goal of **$40,000 before March 31st** to send a loud message about the strength of our campaign and fund the voter outreach efforts needed to send Erica to Congress.
Here’s your official Team Erica donor record for this quarter so far:
**_Your Official 2022 Q1 Donor Record_** **Email:** dpwillis67@gmail.com **Contributed?** Not yet!
**Suggested contribution:** [$5](https://act.ericaforus.com/go/45229?refcode=em20220322-1018&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=1018%2E3698%2EH2pTqA) **[Donate $5](https://act.ericaforus.com/go/45229?refcode=em20220322-1018&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=1018%2E3698%2EH2pTqA)** (_* If you've donated recently, in the mail, or with a different email address, your record might not yet reflect your contribution. Thank you for your support!)_
---
**We’re so grateful you are a part of this team.** We know that times are hard for many — but if you can afford it, we hope you can make your first $5 donation to build out our organizing program, hire staff, and visit all 19 counties in our district.
**With our primary just eight weeks away, we’re relying on grassroots supporters like you to build a winning campaign.**
Erica is a single mom and an educator who grew up on the family farm in rural Eastern North Carolina. She can’t afford to self-fund this campaign or call up wealthy friends to write big checks whenever we need it.
[**The reality is to send Erica to Congress, we need your support. Will you make a gift of $5 to elect Erica to the U.S. House today? Any amount will go a long way.**](https://act.ericaforus.com/go/45229?refcode=em20220322-1018-box&%3Bamp%3Bamounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=1018%2E3698%2EH2pTqA)
**[Donate $5](https://act.ericaforus.com/go/102?refcode=em20220322-1018&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=22&akid=1018%2E3698%2EH2pTqA)**
Thank you,
Team Erica Smith
Erica Smith is a single mother, educator, and three-term state senator, who grew up on her family farm and spent her life fighting for rural Eastern North Carolina. Now, she’s running to represent NC-01 — _her home district_ – in Congress, where she’ll fight for economic justice and workers’ rights, universal healthcare, voting rights, and more. **[Add a donation of $5 (or anything you can today) to help us file a strong Q1 fundraising report, defend this open U.S. House seat, and give power back to working people »](https://act.ericaforus.com/go/45229?refcode=em20220322-1018-box&%3Bamounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=23&akid=1018%2E3698%2EH2pTqA)** **[Donate](https://act.ericaforus.com/go/45229?refcode=em20220322-1018-box&%3Bamounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=24&akid=1018%2E3698%2EH2pTqA)**
---

Our last end-of-quarter deadline before our May 17th primary election is just over a week away — and what we raise will become public for our opponents, the media, and political pundits to see. We’re racing to reach our critical fundraising goal of $40,000 before March 31st to send a loud message about the strength of our campaign and fund the voter outreach efforts needed to send Erica to Congress. Here’s your official Team Erica donor record for this quarter so far: Your Official 2022 Q1 Donor Record Email: dpwillis67@gmail.com Contributed? Not yet! Suggested contribution: ( If you've donated recently, in the mail, or with a different email address, your record might not yet reflect your contribution. Thank you for your support!) We’re so grateful you are a part of this team. We know that times are hard for many — but if you can afford it, we hope you can make your first $5 donation to build out our organizing program, hire staff, and visit all 19 counties in our district. With our primary just eight weeks away, we’re relying on grassroots supporters like you to build a winning campaign. Erica is a single mom and an educator who grew up on the family farm in rural Eastern North Carolina. She can’t afford to self-fund this campaign or call up wealthy friends to write big checks whenever we need it. Thank you, Team Erica Smith Erica Smith is a single mother, educator, and three-term state senator, who grew up on her family farm and spent her life fighting for rural Eastern North Carolina. Now, she’s running to represent NC-01 — her home district – in Congress, where she’ll fight for economic justice and workers’ rights, universal healthcare, voting rights, and more. Email us: mailto: info@ericaforus.com

I'm confident I can be an influential member of Congress for the First District and guide us on a national and worldwide stage under the ideals and principles of what it means to be a proud American. I genuinely care about every one of you, whether you are a democrat, republican, or independent. If elected, I will strive to be the voice for all district residents. My family, friends, and staff have put everything we have into turning our vision of this great state into a reality, and we will continue to work towards achieving that objective. Every day, we build and strengthen partnerships with voters and citizens alike who align with the vision we want to bring to the United States and Northern Michigan. I particularly love meeting and working with the younger generations. They are the voice of the future and are very active and enthusiastic in learning how to be involved. The United States is divided economically, politically, and culturally more than ever before. So, it's time to unite, and it's time to come together and push for government solutions that reflect the best of Northern Michigan's values and priorities. If you make any contribution (including any purchase) on or through the services, your payment information (e.g., credit or debit card type, number, and expiration date) and related information (e.g., physical address) may be collected by us and/or our third-party payment processors. To comply with Federal election law and the regulations of the Federal Election Commission, we are required to do our best to collect and report the name, mailing address, occupation, and name of employer of individuals whose contributions aggregate in excess of $200 per election cycle. Our Apps or other services features may rely on this location information to provide certain location-based services, such as our promotional offers, merchandise offers, event information, or other Save America-related content that may be of interest to you. We may also use location information to improve the functionality of our Apps and our other applications and services. If you do not wish to have this location information collected and used by us, you may opt out by disabling the location and Bluetooth features on your device and/or in our Apps. As a general note, you may choose not to provide certain information, but you may not be able to access certain content or take advantage of certain features of the Services. | | Peter,

Katie Porter is trying to have it both ways.

She’s telling voters in California that she opposes earmarks because they “invite corruption and wasteful backroom deals.”

But at the same time, she just voted for a more than $1 trillion spending bill chock full of earmarks! In fact, the bill included more than 4,000 earmarks totaling 367 pages!

We can’t let her get away with this political double-speak. That’s why I’m launching a petition to expose this fraud. Will you join me in holding her accountable for her lies?
---
| [ ](https://baughforcongress.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c960c16667ac69bd188629a19&id=f6aec9c3ee&e=8a657725d1)
---
| [DIGITALLY SIGN HERE](https://baughforcongress.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c960c16667ac69bd188629a19&id=53b84818bd&e=8a657725d1 "DIGITALLY SIGN HERE")
---
| Peter, the bill included millions of dollars funneled to allies of AOC and other politicians. That’s the definition of a “wasteful backroom deal.”

So, why is Katie Porter lying to voters in California? We must expose the truth!

Thank you,
Scott Baugh

clean_body: | | | | | Peter, Katie Porter is trying to have it both ways. She’s telling voters in California that she opposes earmarks because they “invite corruption and wasteful backroom deals.” But at the same time, she just voted for a more than $1 trillion spending bill chock full of earmarks! In fact, the bill included more than 4,000 earmarks totaling 367 pages! We can’t let her get away with this political double-speak. That’s why I’m launching a petition to expose this fraud. Will you join me in holding her accountable for her lies? | | "DIGITALLY SIGN HERE" | Peter, the bill included millions of dollars funneled to allies of AOC and other politicians. That’s the definition of a “wasteful backroom deal.” So, why is Katie Porter lying to voters in California? We must expose the truth! Thank you, Scott Baugh P.S. Will, you consider making a donation to help us spread the word? Even helps us reach more voters! | | "Paid for by Scott Baugh for Congress. FEC ID# C00798322" | | | | | | | | | | | Copyright © 2022 Scott Baugh for Congress, All rights reserved.

With President Biden and Vice President Harris in the White House, we have a clear path forward. But we can't get complacent. We have to keep fighting if we're going to undo the damage Donald Trump has done. | Together we can protect access to health care, expand Social Security, and fight for vital institutions like the Post Office. Because it's what's right. As [1] of just [3] Democratic physicians in Congress, it's more important than ever that I am able to fight for our most vulnerable communities. disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Indiana Democratic Party, www
body:
we’ve got to keep up the pressure

|
---
Peter, The pressure we’ve been able to put on the state legislature and Governor’s office is working, thanks to support from strong Democrats like you. **Yesterday evening Governor Eric Holcomb vetoed HB1041** — a bill that would have villainized innocent children who wanted to play the sports they enjoy with their friends. The moment the _Indiana High School Athletic Association_ admitted there was no unfair advantage occurring in women’s sports, it became clear what _House Bill 1041_ was about: **national GOP culture wars.** Indiana Republicans even admitted their goal was more about abiding by national politics than solving today’s problems facing kids — _and it’s why they partnered with a national hate group to pass House Bill 1041._ It’s encouraging to see Governor Holcomb tell his party that their culture wars have crossed the line. This is a HUGE victory for Hoosier families — signing House Bill 1041 into law would have put the lives of our children in jeopardy — **but, the fight doesn’t stop and the Governor’s desk.** **The Republicans in the Indiana legislature have the opportunity to override this veto in the next legislative session and reverse Governor Holcomb’s decision to protect trans children.** **[Help the Indiana Democratic Party keep up the pressure and continue to make sure Hoosier children have a bright and welcoming future in Indiana by chipping in today.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43261065/336430578/405560545?refcode=idp-em-220322-hb1041&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5M2E2OWNkOC0zMGFhLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kQo7YhdpknWH5O2N_bEOkIFXyKeVSg_qxBfio1U8s4M=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | [Chip in $10 now ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/43261066/336430579/405560545?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=idp-em-220322-hb1041&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5M2E2OWNkOC0zMGFhLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kQo7YhdpknWH5O2N_bEOkIFXyKeVSg_qxBfio1U8s4M=)
